title: Novavax Q4 2024 and Full Year Results: Strategic Pivot to Partnership Model and Asset Sales
text: Novavax reported full year 2024 revenue of $682 million, down from $984 million in 2023. The company achieved total fourth quarter revenue of $88 million compared to $291 million in the prior year period. Product sales declined significantly to $190 million for the full year (versus $531 million in 2023) and $50 million in Q4 (compared to $251 million in Q4 2023). The decrease reflected lower product sales under advance purchase agreement contracts. The company reported a net loss of $187 million for 2024 versus $545 million in 2023, representing improvement despite lower revenues. R&D expenses decreased to $391 million annually from $738 million, while SG&A expenses fell to $337 million from $469 million. Novavax transitioned commercial leadership of its Nuvaxovid COVID-19 vaccine to Sanofi beginning with the 2025-2026 season. CEO John C. Jacobs stated the company is shifting focus from commercializing the COVID-19 vaccine to maximizing the value of its cutting-edge technology platform. The company completed a $200 million sale of its Czech manufacturing facility to Novo Nordisk, generating a $52 million gain and reducing annual operating costs by approximately $80 million. Novavax projects combined R&D and SG&A expenses between $475-$525 million for 2025, with adjusted licensing and royalty revenue expected between $300-$350 million. The company ended 2024 with over $1 billion in cash and receivables.
